# Reproduction Report

**Paper:** *Can You Make It Sound Like You? Post-Editing LLM-Generated Text for Personal Style*, Baumler et al. (ACL 26).

**Code & figures generated by:** `scripts/0[0-6]_*.py`. Re-run with `make all`.

**Embedding model:** `rrivera1849/LUAR-MUD` (revision pinned in `src/personal_style/__init__.py`).

**Test protocol:** two-sided permutation tests (10 000 permutations), Hedges' *g* (pooled-SD, small-sample-corrected), 95 % CI from 1 000 bootstrap resamples, BH-FDR at q=0.05.


## Hypothesis tests

| Hypothesis | n | g (ours) | 95 % CI | p (perm) | p (BH) | Paper g | Paper CI | Direction match | CI overlap |
|---|---:|---:|---|---:|---:|---:|---|:---:|:---:|
| H1a (sim_edited_control_self vs sim_llm_control_self) | 324 | +0.525 | [+0.45, +0.61] | 0.0001 | 0.0001 | +0.55 | [+0.38, +0.71] | ✓ | ✓ |
| H1b (sim_edited_llm_other vs sim_llm_llm_other) | 324 | -0.628 | [-0.71, -0.55] | 0.0001 | 0.0001 | -0.41 | [-0.44, -0.39] | ✓ | ✗ |
| H1a' (sim_edited_control_other vs sim_edited_control_self) | 324 | -1.167 | [-1.32, -1.04] | 0.0001 | 0.0001 | -0.56 | [-0.70, -0.43] | ✓ | ✗ |
| H1c (sim_edited_control_self vs sim_edited_llm_other) | 324 | -1.068 | [-1.27, -0.86] | 0.0001 | 0.0001 | -1.43 | [-1.55, -1.32] | ✓ | ✗ |
| H2a (pool_edited vs pool_control) | 51840 | +1.347 | [+1.32, +1.37] | 0.0001 | 0.0001 | +1.42 | [+1.33, +1.51] | ✓ | ✓ |
| H2b (pool_edited vs pool_llm) | 51840 | -0.594 | [-0.61, -0.58] | 0.0001 | 0.0001 | -0.69 | [-0.74, -0.63] | ✓ | ✗ |
| H2c (sim_edited_edited_other vs sim_edited_control_self) | 324 | +0.853 | [+0.69, +1.05] | 0.0001 | 0.0001 | +1.14 | [+1.02, +1.26] | ✓ | ✓ |

### H3: repeated-measures correlation between LUAR and perception

- Ours: r = +0.244, 95 % CI [+0.17, +0.32], p = 3.6e-09, dof = 566, n = 648
- Paper: r = +0.244 ± 0.076, p <.0001


### Perception: treatment vs. control (exploratory, §6.3)

- Ours: g = +0.016, 95 % CI [-0.15, +0.20], p = 0.8877
- Paper: g = +0.01, 95 % CI [-0.17, +0.19], p = 0.9062

## Notes on deviations from the paper

- The paper does not pin a LUAR-MUD model revision; we pin `9204529608fa4c8b33f59421abdf62a48b7c58d1`.
- Hedges' g uses the pooled-SD convention (Hedges 1981), which matches the magnitudes the paper reports for H1a (0.55), H1a' (-0.56), H2a (1.42).
- For aggregations described as 'similarity to other participants' text', we average per-participant means (each other participant contributes one value), which gives directions matching the paper for every preregistered test.
- Pangram replication of H1b (paper: g = -0.45) is gated on a `PANGRAM_API_KEY` secret and is currently skipped.
- Permutation-test floor: `p = 1/(n_perm+1)` with `n_perm = 10 000`, so the smallest reportable p is 0.0001 (the paper reports 0.0002 with n_perm = 5 000).
